invariants.condition: All runtime code reads configuration through the centralized config module via explicit getters. No direct process.env access in runtime code.
invariants.assessment: Check for process.env.* usage outside config/index.ts. All env vars should be accessed via exported helpers like getRequiredRpcUrl(), getSupabaseUrl().
invariants.why: Orthodoxy: one obvious way to access config. Discoverability: named getters are searchable, validation errors explain what's missing. Security: schema validation catches missing secrets before deployment.

do: Extend config/index.ts schema when adding new env vars
Use RUNTIME_ENVIRONMENT flag (default, test, review) for runtime overrides
dont: Access process.env directly in runtime code
Introduce new env vars without adding to centralized config

exceptions: One-off scripts or tests may read process.env if documented and not introducing canonical patterns
Transitional compatibility layers inside config module

invariants.condition: Tests, fixtures, and local tooling write generated wallets or secrets only to the canonical temp secret workspace (outside git worktrees) and clean up afterward
invariants.assessment: Check for .operate-test/ or similar secret directories created beneath tracked repositories. All ephemeral secrets should go to OS temp directory.
invariants.why: Automation that stages 'everything' cannot accidentally pick up secret fixtures. IDEs and commit UIs never surface sensitive files inside tracked trees.

do: Use mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'jinn-operate-test-')) to create temp workspace
Inject absolute path via OPERATE_HOME environment variable
Clean up secrets after test completes
dont: Use join(process.cwd(), '.operate-test') which creates secrets in git worktree
Leave test secrets behind after tests complete

invariants.condition: All runtime HTTP calls use the shared client module with timeouts, structured errors, and retry/backoff. No direct fetch() calls.
invariants.assessment: Check for direct fetch() calls in runtime code. All HTTP should use postJson, getJson, graphQLRequest from http/client.ts.
invariants.why: Orthodoxy: one pattern for HTTP keeps formatting, logging, and error handling consistent. Discoverability: named helpers are searchable. Security: timeouts prevent hangs, retries smooth failures, centralized logging prevents secret leaks.

do: const data = await graphQLRequest<Response>({ query, variables, context: { requestId } });
Extend http/client.ts for new behavior (streaming) so protections apply
Use helper options: requestId, headers, timeoutMs, retries
dont: const res = await fetch(url, { method: 'POST', body }); // no timeout
Implement custom retry logic outside the shared client
Call fetch directly in runtime code

exceptions: Unit tests in controlled environments with documented deviation

invariants.condition: Auto-commit or auto-push helpers call the canonical staged-tree validator immediately before committing, enforcing the shared denylist
invariants.assessment: Check all auto-commit/push paths. Verify gitGuard.ensureSafeStagedTree() is called after staging and before committing.
invariants.why: Auto-commit paths bypass human review--the guard is the only defense. Centralized checks keep denylist synchronized. Failures clearly identify offending paths.

do: await git.add({ all: true }); await gitGuard.ensureSafeStagedTree(); await git.commit({ message });
Propagate guard errors to abort workflow with context
Update shared denylist when new patterns emerge
dont: await git.add({ all: true }); await git.commit({ message }); // no guard

invariants.condition: All runtime code logs through the shared Pino logger. Components create child loggers with component tags and use structured metadata. No console.* in production code.
invariants.assessment: Check for console.log/warn/error in runtime code. All logging should use logger from logging/index.ts with child loggers per component.
invariants.why: Orthodoxy: one logging pipeline keeps formatting and routing consistent. Discoverability: structured fields (requestId, jobId) make logs searchable. Security: centralized logging prevents accidental secret leakage.

do: Keep sensitive values out of log fields
dont: Log concatenated strings instead of structured objects
Include private keys or tokens in log fields

exceptions: Browser-facing code with devtools logging not reaching production

invariants.condition: Every catch block surfaces the error. Optional flows log warning with context; critical flows log and rethrow. No empty catch blocks or error-suppressing .catch() patterns.
invariants.assessment: Scan for empty catch {} blocks, .catch(() => ...) without logging. Every catch must log or rethrow.
invariants.why: Orthodoxy: single discoverable error-handling idiom. Discoverability: logs provide breadcrumbs for future agents. Security: prevents silent data loss or hidden failures.

do: catch (error) { workerLogger.error({ requestId, error: serializeError(error) }, 'Failed'); throw error; }
Non-critical paths: log at warn level and continue
Use serializeError() for readable error details without leaking secrets
dont: return null; // in catch without logging cause

exceptions: Test helpers deliberately swallowing errors during cleanup (documented)
Inline parse guards with explicit fallback: try { JSON.parse(...) } catch { return null; } (tolerated if documented)
